assessments.rationale: NFPA 101 Life Safety Code imposes the identical single-motion egress principle as EN 179/EN 1125. Product's mechanical design inherently satisfies the underlying requirement; no friction with CCRC regulatory environment.

CMS Conditions of Participation and NFPA Life Safety Code require life-safety systems to operate under power failure. Product's mechanical-primary architecture operates at 0V — this constraint is pre-satisfied and reinforces the product's value proposition.

Average CCRC campus spans multiple wings with fire-rated construction in memory care units. 30m base range insufficient for long corridor runs; repeater mesh and per-cluster gateways provide full coverage — standard mitigation with no engineering novelty.
assessments.mitigation: RF site survey to map signal attenuation zones across campus wings; Deploy wireless repeaters every 30m in corridors and at floor transitions; Install dedicated BUS gateway per care-level cluster (MC, AL, SNF)

assessments.rationale: Dementia residents frequently test exit doors, creating high event frequency that exhausts 9V battery below the 2-year target. Supervised-access requirement in secured MCUs makes unplanned battery replacement operationally difficult. Battery-status alerts and scheduled maintenance cycles fully mitigate.
assessments.mitigation: Enable wireless battery-status alerts for proactive replacement dispatch; Add quarterly battery checks to facility maintenance calendar; Specify high-capacity or low-power variant for memory care exits

assessments.rationale: CCRC environments prioritize functional safety over architectural aesthetics. Surface-mounted safety hardware is standard and expected in institutional care settings across all care levels.

CCRC nurse-call panels (Stanley Healthcare 40%, hardwired Jeron 35%) are the clinical destination for elopement alerts. EasyWave's proprietary protocol requires a GfS BUS gateway with relay output to bridge into nurse-call — adds hardware and integration effort per cluster but is architecturally straightforward.
assessments.mitigation: Deploy GfS wireless BUS gateway with potential-free contact output to nurse-call panel auxiliary input; Engage nurse-call vendor (Stanley Healthcare, Jeron) for integration wiring spec and alarm category mapping; Evaluate GfS IP gateway for direct BMS/EHR event logging if required

assessments.rationale: Elopement liability exposure (wrongful death suits, CMS penalties, increased staffing) far exceeds the EUR 730/door premium. Professional B2B procurement in regulated care is ROI-driven; the liability case is unambiguous. Cost is never a knockout.

CCRCs are US-based institutions (~2,000 in US); GDPR does not apply. Door-event data may implicate HIPAA PHI rules under patient location data interpretation, but falls within existing HIPAA compliance infrastructure — no new regulatory framework required.

CCRC emergency exits are in heated indoor corridors, stairwells, and ground-floor vestibule doors. No washdown, chemical splash, or extreme humidity exposure. Standard IP class is sufficient for all deployment locations.

~30% of CCRC exits use electromagnetic delayed-egress locks (CMS permits 30-sec delay in AL/MC). Combined Türwächter + EM lock systems must maintain fail-safe escape release. System-level design compliance is well-understood; commissioning test and AHJ acceptance are standard.
assessments.mitigation: Design EM lock to release on fire alarm signal and on power failure simultaneously with Türwächter alarm activation; Verify fail-safe release sequencing during commissioning with AHJ inspection; Document combined system compliance with NFPA 101
